claim: 9 damage operators form closed algebra with TRUNCATE as universal sink
evidence:
RERUN after depth2_matrix rebuild. depth3_probes is UNCHANGED.
Prefix (op1) distribution across 14 crack probes:
  HIERARCHIZE: 7/14 (50.0%)
  TRUNCATE: 4/14 (28.6%)
  PARTITION: 2/14 (14.3%)
  EXTEND: 1/14 (7.1%)
TRUNCATE as prefix: 4/14 = 28.6% (need >90% for PASS)
TRUNCATE anywhere in chain: 9/14 = 64.3%
HIERARCHIZE dominates as prefix at 50.0%, TRUNCATE is dominant as interior op.
depth2_matrix rebuild: operators now show differentiated confidence profiles.
TRUNCATE as op1 HIGH cells: 321. Max HIGH op1: INVERT with 432.
Rebuild does NOT change this result -- driven by depth3_probes (raw).
rebuild_note: depth2_matrix confidence profiles now differentiated. TRUNCATE has distinct confidence pattern from other operators. But the depth3 prefix distribution still shows HIERARCHIZE > TRUNCATE.
implications_for_other_papers: TRUNCATE remains the most common damage operator in cross-domain edges, confirming its role as a frequent resolution mechanism. But it is NOT a universal sink/prefix at depth 3. HIERARCHIZE is the dominant prefix, suggesting meta-level reasoning (going up a level) is the primary resolution strategy, with TRUNCATE as the secondary operation applied within.
